title: taraxacum angulare
intro: Taraxacum angulare, also known as the angular dandelion or rough dandelion, is a type of plant that belongs to the dandelion genus and is commonly found in Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve liver and gallbladder problems, and reduce inflammation. The plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like potassium and iron, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and well-being. Taraxacum angulare can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
